mechanism: A computational learning system detects patterns in sequential input by calculating statistics over event co-occurrences. Attention selectively amplifies representations of attended items. The learning output encodes associations between amplified items, yielding capacity-limited pattern detection. Unattended items remain suppressed regardless of objective contiguity.

A single familiarity signal arises from memory encoding. This signal is scaled differently and subjected to independent noise sources for different tasks. Recognition judgment applies a criterion to the noisy signal; response latency decreases with signal magnitude. Dissociations between tasks arise from differential noise rather than separate memory sources.

A processing system with two selectivity modes: active inhibition (dedicated inhibitory control suppresses competing representations) versus reactive inhibition (goal-enhancement of target codes indirectly suppresses competitors via lateral inhibition). Task performance depends on which mode or combination is engaged.

Semantic relations in priming context modulate automatic spreading of activation across a memory network. When semantic structure aligns with arithmetic operation structure, categorically organized primes enhance activation of operation-relevant facts. Misaligned semantic structures suppress or block automatic activation.

Activation cascades continuously from conceptual representations through lemmas to phonological forms in a hierarchical lexical network. The magnitude of activation spreading is modulated by task goal and attentional selection. When task requires concept selection (picture naming), full cascade occurs; when task bypasses conceptual access (word reading), cascade is limited.

A capacity-limited memory buffer stores items with distributed attention. When attention focuses on a single item, that item becomes consolidated and resistant to interference from new input. Without focused attention, all items remain vulnerable to overwriting by incoming stimuli. Task-dependent attention gates consolidation strength.
